heading: Regulations
label: 2436 Regulations
notes: Amendments2015— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 114–81

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of authorities, functions, personnel, and assets of the Coast Guard, including the authorities and functions of the Secretary of Transportation relating thereto, to the Department of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 468(b), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6
amendments: Amendments2015— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 114–81

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce, after consultation with the Secretary of State, the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating, and the heads of other appropriate departments or agencies of the United States, shall promulgate such regulations as are necessary and appropriate to implement the provisions of this chapter.

children.heading: Regulations to implement conservation measures
children.label: b Regulations to implement conservation measures
A
children.text: that has been in effect for 12 months or less;

children.label: B
children.text: that is adopted by the Commission; and

children.label: C
children.text: with respect to which the Secretary of State, does not notify Commission in accordance with within the time period allotted for objections under Article IX of the Convention.12section 2434(a)(1) of this titleSo in original. The comma probably should not appear.1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “the”.2

children.heading: Entering into force
children.label: 2 Entering into force
children.text: Upon publication of such regulation in the Federal Register, such conservation measure shall enter into force with respect to the United States.
